critic_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. A genomics startup trained a deep sequence model to predict whether missense variants in an inherited cardiomyopathy panel are pathogenic...

CRITIQUE:

1. [PANEL-SPECIFIC-VS-GENERIC-THRESHOLDING] PANEL-SPECIFIC vs. GENERIC THRESHOLDING: The startup's model was calibrated on cardiomyopathy variants; PolyPhen-2 uses its generic published threshold. Threshold optimization at domain-specific calibration is a direct scoring advantage unrelated to model quality.

2. [HOTSPOT-GENE-COMPOSITION-ADVANTAGE] HOTSPOT GENE COMPOSITION ADVANTAGE: 40% of the benchmark comes from two sarcomere genes with exceptionally well-characterized hotspots. These are the easiest variants to classify correctly. Performance on this subset may drive the AUROC gap, and it may not generalize beyond these well-annotated genes.

3. [LIMITED-BASELINE-SET-FOR-MODERN-CLAIM] LIMITED BASELINE SET FOR MODERN CLAIM: The claim is that 'modern foundation-model representations outperform classical variant-effect predictors.' The only baseline is PolyPhen-2. No modern deep-learning pathogenicity tools (AlphaMissense, EVE, ESM-1v) are included. This comparison cannot support the claimed foundation-model advantage.
defender_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. A genomics startup trained a deep sequence model to predict whether missense variants in an inherited cardiomyopathy panel are pathogenic...

DEFENSE:

Domain-specific calibration reflects the intended deployment. PolyPhen-2 is the clinically deployed comparator. Cardiomyopathy hotspot genes represent real clinical burden. The benchmark quality is high (curated ClinVar labels, binary classification).
adjudication_raw: Defender appeals to deployment context but does not address the analytical validity of the three issues. Threshold calibration asymmetry, hotspot gene composition advantage, and inadequate baseline set for the stated claim are all structural problems that inflate the apparent AUROC gap. The paper's framing of 'foundation-model representations outperform classical predictors' is particularly problematic given only one circa-2002 tool is compared. Critique wins.
